All right, welcome back. In this lesson, we're going to talk about automated conversion ecosystems or what I like to call Newtonian versus quantum marketing. So this is a term that we're going to use throughout the program, and this is where I really unpack exactly what I mean when I say it and what's going on and how that really maps onto our compares to what is typically known as a sales funnel. So before we get into that, what is the problem with what I call Newtonian marketing, which is also known as linear funnels that assume someone has consumed every email in a sequence, watched all the videos in your product launch formula video series, and heard all the arguments and belief shifts that you had to offer and watched a full webinar. So this is a linear funnel. You'll see all those lines and boxes, and when a marketer talks about it, they'll essentially say, first, our prospect will see this, then they'll listen to that, then they'll believe that. And then finally, at the end of it, they will have bought based on all these things. Well, the truth is, is it doesn't mimic real world behavior. It tells a false story. And therefore, there's been kind of this sneaky little lie in the marketing world that we kind of tell ourselves. And that's that our best practices are actually based on totally false assumptions. So a customer doesn't actually ascend or become a customer in a linear way. Doing so would be like forcing a person to adapt to a funnel, not having a funnel adapt to a person. And that's why 2% is considered a good conversion rate when 2% really is the minority. It's like the smallest fraction of a fraction of people who are consuming your marketing actually becoming customers. So to me, that isn't a good benchmark. And the reason it's so low is that our marketing is forcing a consumer or a prospect or a lead to adapt to it versus us adapting to how people actually transform. And this really is about transformation. A purchase at the end of the day is a transformation. And transformation is not Newtonian. Someone may hear of you, read a post, talk to a friend, and the problem becomes suddenly more acute in their life than they watch a video from a competitor, than they see an ad for a different type of solution. So all these things essentially transform your buyer and they're not linear. They can happen fast. They can happen slower. They can happen to whatever degree your prospect is in an environment that is feeding him or her information. So transformation is not Newtonian, yet marketing still is in many ways. Email funnels and Newtonian marketing still work. This isn't heating on them. But we're still building sequences where different arguments are made across in different assets and we're still operating with the false assumption that they'll all be read in order for our customer or our prospect, sorry, to get exposed to all the reasons why they need to buy from us. So this is still the best way to write email on static assets like sales pages and VSLs and it still works, but it's incomplete and mostly ineffective unless synchronized with more adaptable human based behaviorally triggered touch points. So linear funnels to me are considered the scaffolding. There's a lot of structural integrity that comes from them, but it's an incomplete system in and of itself if we really want to move anywhere beyond these low conversion rates which have been considered normal. So the actual conversion process is quantum and exponential. So on the linear incremental slot, incremental side adds content marketing that's linear within our control. Email marketing is linear. Webinar, sales pages, VSLs all linear where we build our arguments. They have a very linear incremental structure and that's your typical normal funnel that will convert anywhere between not converting at all and 3% which would be considered stellar in many cases. Whereas the actual conversion experience is quantum and exponential things like a prospect's intimacy with pain and problem is not something that is linear or incremental. Our emotions are not linear and incremental. They shift rapidly. They shift as the environment provides us with different situations to emote differently. So our intimacy with pain, problem, desire, all that is quantum and exponential and less predictable than a marketing funnel. Information gathering, comparison that as well does not happen in a linear way. Someone might not do any research for a problem they've had for over a year and then something happens where they're more intimate with that pain or problem very suddenly and all they can think about is that problem and they go down that Google rabbit hole and spend the next four hours consuming everything they can about it. All of a sudden they have moved up those levels of awareness in a very exponential quantum way. Whereas people's marketing is still linear. Motivation, trust, resonance, courage to act, essentially all these precursors that get someone to buy from you, that is quantum and exponential. Someone's motivation and trust doesn't have an incremental growth curve to it. It has an exponential curve to it and our marketing has to adapt to it. So these are the more quantum exponential aspects that underlie what we call an automated conversion ecosystem and the goal in blending that with the structural integrity of a linear and incremental system like the funnel is to get those conversion rates high to 5 to 15%, something that would be considered almost unheard of in most marketing circles. But it's only unheard of because we're still operating in a linear and Newtonian model for an experience that is not linear and Newtonian. All right, so the bottom line is conversion is dynamic and fluid, not fixed and linear and because all of this is going on, it's important that we win the intimacy game to always remain closer, more responsive, more adaptable and more top of mind for our prospect as they're undergoing that transformation. And where we're really of service as what I call a conversion coach is when we're actually the ones who ascend a prospect into a new sense of awareness, understanding, possibility, desire, courage, motivation. All these virtues we talked about on that quantum side. So the takeaway funnels aren't reality based. They don't offer conclusive information. You know, if a prospect clears your funnel, it doesn't mean they were a non buyer. I mean, literally they didn't buy, but they're not a non buyer at the heart of it. It just means their life didn't revolve around a four day countdown timer. So that's the way that webinar hit them. Their four year old interrupted him because he wants to play trucks or baseball or whatever he wants to play. And then they go into family time on the weekend. So he doesn't really care about your weekend bonus 24 hour extra bonus offer, gets a few sales emails, opens it, but then has to cook dinner. Quick glance at the sales page, very distracted while everyone else is trying to essentially buy for his attention. And that's the actual reality of how a prospect may be consuming your marketing and ultimately clearing that funnel, giving you the impression that they were a non buyer when really it was just the context that didn't favor it. So data only ever tells that split second story. Just because someone clears a funnel or didn't respond doesn't mean they're dead as a lot of marketers think they do. It often means they were distracted, overwhelmed or not empowered, inspired or coached into taking decisive action. And there's just so much money left on that table by casting aside dead leads when really it's our marketing that wasn't alive and responsive enough to make an impact. All right, so Uncle Jean, not actually Uncle Jean, but Jean Schwartz needs a modern day facelift too. So many marketers, direct response copywriters are aware of the stages of awareness. So level one, we have unaware, then problem aware, then solution aware, then product aware and most aware. And this is an incredible model that has really stood the test of time going on 50 or 60 years now, something crazy like that. But it does need a facelift because pre digital, this ascension of levels of awareness could take weeks or months. And in 2021 and beyond, the ascension to buyer readiness can happen literally in a 40 minute Uber ride. So Jean Schwartz's levels of awareness, which was written in the 60s, presupposes that marketers control the frame. There was less media, it was TV and print. And with controlled media and less media moving beyond those stages was exponentially slower. Today with social media ads on every page, every channel, retargeting direct messages, that ascension from problem aware to solution aware to product aware can literally happen in a matter of seconds, a few swipes and clicks on your phone, a few Google searches, drop pixels retargeting, then they get competitor ads because that company is targeting those who've liked the other page. Then they have seven different Chrome tabs all open with competitors and it just becomes super overwhelming. Very, very fast. So the thing to understand here is the biggest obstacle to decisiveness and purchase is no longer awareness. It's actually overwhelm, decision fatigue, plus argument dilution across so many competitors all vying for your attention leads to overwhelm, which leads to an action. So you can no longer differentiate just with a better headline or even a better offer right now, you actually need to create a better, safer, more involved and more adaptable conversion experience. And buyers will ultimately gravitate towards coaches or brands that have learned to intervene at the right time with the right message to make them feel safe, excited and tapped into what is possible. So you have two choices. You can either go all blunt force and be omnipresent via sheer force of will and budget on Google display ads, YouTube ads, Facebook, to make sure they never forget about you. Or you can go deeper and get more intimate with every prospect who enters your ecosystem. So the takeaway here, a funnel alone is mechanistic, a.k.a. linear and not aligned with a true buying experience, whereas an automated conversion ecosystem with what I call strategic conversion coaching is humanistic, adaptable, fluid, quantum scalable, but most importantly possible based on the technologies we have access to literally right now. So two components of an automated conversion ecosystem. It's the coaching, the conversion ethos, which is all about the messaging and the transformation marketing. We're going to get into that in just a second. And then component two is automated intimacy, which is our really the mechanics, the intelligence, the systems that empower and enable us to do component one at some degree of scale. So future of profitable funnels, our benchmarks have become too low, five to ten dollar per subscriber per lead. And that could be a lot higher or a little lower, depending on what industry you're in. Sub 2% conversion rates, which are not going to trend up with current methods, more saturation in the online education and coaching and service provider space. So we need to take the best of funnels and meld it into a more holistic, adaptable, scalable, and ultimately profitable paradigm. So that is the quantum leap into automated conversion ecosystems. And in the next few lessons, we're going to talk more about the ethos that automated intimacy is built upon. And then the rest of the program, we're going to actually be building out these automated conversion ecosystems to support each stage of your traditional funnel. Cool. So we'll leave you with this on the linear side. We have the typical funnel, top funnel, middle funnel, bottom funnel. On the right, we're going to be melding that into these aces. So ace one will match to top ace two to middle, ace three to bottom. And these really incorporate some of those more quantum elements we spoke about, things like resonance, things like accountability, things like desire, things like motivation, things like courage. And what we'll be doing is we're going to be using playbooks in each one of these aces to really make sure that we're coaching those virtues, coaching those aspects, coaching those states that are more quantum in nature. So a lot of fun on the way for now. Let's take a breather and I'll meet you soon in that next lesson.
